description: Define the operation-neutral relationship authorization contract layer in Backend.Plans and the request-scoped relational authorization context in Backend.External so shared relationship authorization code no longer depends on GET-many-specific naming or AuthorizationStrategyEvaluator.
acceptance-criteria: Backend.Plans owns configured-strategy, relationship authorization result, subject, check-spec, parameterization, and failure metadata records, and internal Backend adapts legacy evaluator input into those records before planning begins.

RelationalAuthorizationContext becomes operation-neutral, still normalizes and deterministically sorts ClaimEducationOrganizationIds, and also carries deduped ordinal-sorted NamespacePrefixes without changing current GET-many behavior.

The shared failure contract includes the cases Slice 1 can emit plus future values such as stored-null and proposed-missing so later slices reuse one taxonomy instead of introducing parallel result shapes.
Existing relational query request and handler code compiles against the revised contracts while non-GET endpoint behavior and result surfaces remain unchanged in this slice.

description: Thread the typed relational authorization context and raw configured strategy order through the core-to-backend request pipeline so duplicate relationship strategies survive intact into the new shared planner.
acceptance-criteria: Relational query request creation builds RelationalAuthorizationContext from ClientAuthorizations instead of re-parsing ClaimEducationOrganizationIds from per-strategy filters.

The relational request shape preserves raw configured strategy order, duplicate configured strategy names, and NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired entries until the shared classifier determines what is effective.
Internal Backend creates a Plans-owned configured-strategy record with raw configured index and composition metadata before invoking shared planning code.
GET-many keeps the current staged behavior for unsupported non-GET relationship operations; Slice 1 does not wire new CRUD execution behavior.

description: Replace the GET-many-only relationship strategy classifier with an operation-neutral classifier that preserves configured identity and distinguishes supported, staged, no-op, and invalid relationship strategy outcomes.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly and RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted classify into shared EdOrg relationship strategy entries without deduplicating duplicate configured strategies that must remain separate OR branches.

Known People relationship strategies, NamespaceBased, OwnershipBased, and convention-matching custom-view strategies whose basis resolves classify deterministically as known-but-not-enabled metadata instead of generic unknown failures.
Invalid custom-view strategy names, custom-view names whose basis does not resolve, and other invalid security metadata classify as security-configuration failures with deterministic ordering.

When classification finds both true security-configuration errors and known-but-not-enabled strategies, the classified result preserves both metadata sets while marking security-configuration as the winning downstream outcome.

NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired remains in the classified result with raw configured index metadata, emits no check spec, and yields an explicit no-further-authorization outcome when it is the only effective strategy.
Classification preserves both raw configured strategy index and relationship-local order metadata so later CRUD slices can assign dense AUTH1 indexes without losing original configuration identity.

description: Refactor EdOrg subject resolution into an injected cached service that resolves securable-element paths once and then builds CRUD-safe root-table subject metadata for the shared relationship auth core.
acceptance-criteria: The static EdOrg securable-element resolution cache is replaced by a DI-registered singleton or equivalent injected service that caches raw resolution by mapping set or effective schema, resource, and securable element.

CRUD EdOrg subject selection accepts only DbTableKind.Root bindings, excluding child-table and RootExtension candidates from participating authorization subjects while preserving their metadata for diagnostics.

Unresolved elements, child-only elements, and no-applicable-root-subject scenarios aggregate typed security-configuration metadata with resource identity, strategy identity, readable securable names, JSON paths, and deterministic ordering.

When multiple securable elements resolve to the same physical root-table column, the shared model groups them under one predicate or binding while preserving all contributing readable names and paths in deterministic order.

description: Build shared EdOrg relationship authorization specs that emit stored-value inputs, proposed-value binding locators, no-claims metadata, and dialect-aware claim parameterization from the same strategy and subject model without generating endpoint SQL.
acceptance-criteria: The shared core emits stored-value check inputs for existing root documents and proposed-value check inputs that identify the canonical root TableWritePlan binding location, logical key, table and column binding, and stable parameter seed so later slices can generate single-record EXISTS checks without allocating final SQL parameter names in Slice 1.

The shared relationship authorization spec model preserves OR composition across configured strategies and AND composition across grouped EdOrg subjects inside one strategy, while same-column contributor dedupe happens only within a single strategy entry.
Missing proposed root bindings are reported as security-configuration metadata in Slice 1 instead of being deferred to later write-operation slices.
Normal versus inverted EdOrg hierarchy direction is explicit metadata on the shared spec so downstream SQL generation never branches on raw strategy strings.

ClaimEducationOrganizationIds parameterization preserves the DMS-1055 contract: deduped and sorted ids, one PostgreSQL bigint-array parameter, SQL Server scalar bigint parameters below 2000 unique ids, and dms.BigIntTable at 2000 or more unique ids.
The shared core exposes an explicit no-claims result shape for callers to short-circuit before parameterization, and it does not cache or emit operation-specific CRUD SQL in this slice.

description: Migrate the DMS-1055 GET-many authorization path to consume the shared relationship auth core through a page-query adapter instead of owning separate classifier, subject-selection, and failure-shaping logic.
acceptance-criteria: RelationalDocumentStoreRepository builds PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpec from shared relationship authorization specs and shared failure metadata rather than from GET-many-only contracts.

The page-query adapter preserves duplicate configured relationship strategies, OR ordering, and per-strategy identity while deduping repeated physical predicates only after the shared spec has grouped same-column contributors.

Known-but-not-enabled and security-configuration results from the shared core adapt back to current GET-many query failure surfaces without reintroducing page-query-specific naming into the shared contracts.

PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpec remains a page-query adapter with only the minimal order or index fields needed for duplicate-order regression coverage, and non-GET staged behavior remains deferred to later slices.

description: Backfill focused unit coverage for the operation-neutral relationship auth core so classifier, subject-resolution, failure-metadata, and shared check-spec behavior are locked down before later CRUD slices consume it.
acceptance-criteria: Unit coverage exists for supported and inverted EdOrg classification, known-but-not-enabled strategy classification, invalid custom-view configuration failures, duplicate configured strategies, NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired handling, and aggregated configuration-versus-staged outcomes.

Unit coverage exists for root-only subject selection, child-only and no-subject configuration failures, same-column contributor grouping, stored versus proposed spec generation, missing proposed binding failures, no-claims handling, and claim parameterization threshold behavior.
Unit coverage proves the shared spec preserves strategy OR ordering, grouped-subject AND semantics, and duplicate-strategy identity without relying on GET-many-specific types or page-query adapters.

description: Backfill focused request-pipeline and adapter-path coverage for the migrated DMS-1055 GET-many flow so the shared core feeds the existing page-query behavior without reintroducing GET-many-only contracts into the reusable layer.
acceptance-criteria: Focused coverage proves relational request creation sources claim and namespace context from ClientAuthorizations, preserves duplicate configured relationship strategies and raw configured order, and keeps current staged non-GET behavior unchanged in Slice 1.

Adapter-path coverage proves duplicate strategy order, same-column dedupe after shared grouping, and adaptation back to current PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpec and query-failure surfaces without copying rich shared-core failure contracts into the page-query model.

GET-many unit coverage asserts current result-kind or status behavior for known-but-not-enabled, security-configuration, no-further-authorization, and empty-claims outcomes remains aligned with DMS-1055 after the migration.

description: Run the minimal existing DMS-1055 PostgreSQL, SQL Server, and focused DMS E2E regression surface needed to prove the migrated GET-many adapter preserved behavior, and add new integration coverage only if the refactor exposes an uncovered adapter-specific risk.
acceptance-criteria: Existing PostgreSQL and SQL Server DMS-1055 backend integration coverage passes against the migrated GET-many path for normal, inverted, OR, empty-claims, and parameterization behavior.

Any new integration coverage added here is narrowly limited to adapter-specific risks such as duplicate configured strategies, mixed no-op strategies, same-column dedupe, or aggregated configuration failures rather than Slice 2-6 CRUD execution behavior.

Focused DMS E2E regressions validate real token and claim wiring for the migrated GET-many path after a clean dms-local teardown and setup, without introducing new CRUD scenarios owned by later slices.
